purpose: Per-dashboard project-path and per-dashboard additional-context-directory mappings persisted to localStorage with optional async sync to disk via window.electronAPI.saveAdditionalContext / getAdditionalContext. Each dashboard is bound to a target project (the {project_root} for that swarm) and may also have additional context dirs (extra read-only roots given to workers). The disk-sync layer (loadDashboardAdditionalContextFromDisk / loadAllDashboardAdditionalContextFromDisk) merges localStorage with disk-persisted data so dirs survive a localStorage clear.
gotchas: saveDashboardAdditionalContext fire-and-forgets the disk sync (.catch(() => {})) — disk save failures are silently swallowed.

Disk-load merge produces a UNION of localStorage and disk dirs (deduped) — there's no canonical 'one source of truth'; localStorage is always written but may be missing dirs that exist only on disk until loadAllDashboardAdditionalContextFromDisk runs.
saveDashboardProject does NOT trigger disk-sync — only additional context has the disk-sync layer; project paths live in localStorage only.
All getters parse JSON.parse on every call — high-frequency callers should memoize.
clearDashboardProject early-exits when key already absent — no-op, no localStorage write avoided.
STORAGE_KEY 'synapse-dashboard-projects' and ADDITIONAL_CONTEXT_KEY 'synapse-dashboard-additional-context' must not collide with other 'synapse-' keys.
patterns: Pure localStorage helpers — no React state. Components call these directly and re-render via dispatch when they care.
Disk-sync as opt-in side-effect (only when window.electronAPI.saveAdditionalContext exists) — keeps utility usable in standalone-server mode.
conventions: Uses 'synapse-' prefix on all storage keys.
Try/catch around localStorage on read; quota errors swallowed on write.
